method: Time-varying beta sensitivity, 200,000-draw Monte Carlo with antithetic variates; beta(t) declines linearly to a target by 2040 and is held flat to 2050.
headline: Even under a 50% attenuation of the migration elasticity by 2040, the present-value depopulation cost remains in the lower half of the original $11-38B 90% interval (median $14B). Under a 75% attenuation, the median drops to $7B. The headline interval is therefore conservative against plausible regime-change attenuation.
